name: Naryn-Kala Citadel
description: Russia's oldest fortress (4th–6th c.) and the core of the city of Derbent. A UNESCO World Heritage Site.

name: Sulak Canyon
description: One of the deepest canyons in the world (up to 1920 m) with the turquoise Sulak River at its bottom.

name: Gamsutl Ghost Village
description: An abandoned mountain village on a ridge top, the "Dagestani Machu Picchu", about 2000 years old.

name: Sarykum Dune
description: The largest sand dune in Europe, over 250 metres high, standing in the middle of the steppe.

name: Saltinsky Waterfall
description: The only underground waterfall in Dagestan, plunging into the Saltinsky gorge.

name: Gunib
description: A historic mountain village and fortress, where the Caucasian War ended in 1859.

name: Karadakh Gorge
description: A narrow gorge, the "Gate of Wonders", with sheer walls up to 170 metres high.

name: Erzi Tower Complex
description: One of the largest medieval tower complexes in Ingushetia — dozens of battle and dwelling towers.

name: Vovnushki
description: A castle complex of battle towers growing straight out of the rocks. A finalist of the "Seven Wonders of Russia".

name: Egikal
description: The largest ancient tower town of Ingushetia and the ancestral home of many Ingush clans.

name: Thaba-Yerdy Temple
description: The oldest Christian temple in the North Caucasus (8th–9th c.), a monument of Alan architecture.

name: Dzheyrakh Gorge
description: A picturesque mountain gorge — a reserve with towers, sanctuaries and alpine meadows.

name: Lyazhgi Waterfall
description: A two-cascade waterfall about 18 metres high surrounded by pine forest.

name: Heart of Chechnya Mosque
description: One of the largest mosques in Europe, named after Akhmat Kadyrov, in the centre of Grozny.

name: Lake Kezenoy-Am
description: The largest and deepest high-mountain lake of the North Caucasus, at 1870 metres.

name: Argun Gorge
description: A grand gorge along the Argun River with dozens of medieval towers and fortresses.

name: Ushkaloy Towers
description: Twin watchtowers built right into an overhanging cliff above the Argun River.

name: Tsoy-Pede
description: An ancient "city of the dead" — the largest necropolis in Chechnya, with crypts and battle towers.

name: Nikhaloy Waterfalls
description: A cascade of waterfalls in the Argun Gorge with an equipped eco-trail.

name: Mount Elbrus
description: The highest peak of Russia and Europe (5642 m), a dormant volcano and a hub of mountain tourism.

name: Blue Lakes (Tserik-Kel)
description: A karst lake with deep-blue water and a depth of more than 250 metres.

name: Chegem Waterfalls
description: A group of waterfalls plunging from the cliffs of the gorge; in winter they turn into ice columns.

name: Upper Balkaria
description: An ancient mountain society with the ruins of clan towers and castles on the gorge slopes.

name: El-Tyubyu (City of the Dead)
description: An old village with the Balkarukov tower and a medieval crypt necropolis.

name: Azau Glade
description: A high-mountain glade at 2300 m — the start of the cable cars and ski slopes of Elbrus.

name: Dombay
description: A famous mountain resort surrounded by the peaks of the Main Caucasus Range.

name: Arkhyz
description: A mountain valley with lakes, pine forests and some of the clearest skies for astronomy.

name: Nizhny Arkhyz Temples
description: Alan Christian temples of the 10th century — the oldest in Russia.

name: Sofia Waterfalls
description: Powerful waterfalls flowing from the glaciers of Mount Sofia, dropping about 90 metres.

name: Teberda Reserve
description: A biosphere reserve with a hundred mountain lakes, glaciers and bison.

name: Honey Waterfalls
description: A cascade of five waterfalls in the Alikonovka gorge near Kislovodsk.

name: Dargavs City of the Dead
description: The largest medieval necropolis in the Caucasus — nearly a hundred stone crypt tombs.

name: Tsey Gorge
description: A picturesque gorge with glaciers, the Rekom sanctuary and the Tsey mountain resort.

name: Midagrabin Waterfalls
description: Among the highest waterfalls in Russia and Europe — the Greater Zeygelan falls from over 600 metres.

name: Dzivgis Rock Fortress
description: A unique medieval fortress built into the natural caves of a sheer cliff.

name: Kurtatinsky Gorge
description: One of the most beautiful gorges of Ossetia, with towers, a monastery and mountain ravines.

name: Rekom Sanctuary
description: An ancient wooden Ossetian sanctuary, one of the main sacred sites of the traditional religion.
